back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Command the sons of Israel that they bring to you pure beaten olive oil for the light, to cause the lamp to burn continually.
Outside the veil of the testimony in the tent of meeting, Aaron shall arrange it from evening until morning before the Lord continually. It is a permanent statute throughout your generations.
Upon the pure lampstand he shall arrange the lamps before the Lord continually.
And you shall take fine flour and bake it into twelve loaves; two-tenths shall be in each loaf.
And you shall set them in two rows, six in the row, upon the pure table before the Lord.
And you shall place upon each row pure frankincense, and it shall be for the bread as a memorial portion, a fire offering to the Lord.
On the Sabbath day, on the Sabbath day, he shall arrange it before the Lord continually, from the sons of Israel as a permanent covenant.
And it shall be for Aaron and for his sons, and they shall eat it in a holy place, for it is most holy to him from the fire offerings of the Lord—a permanent statute.
And the son of an Israelite woman went out—he was the son of an Egyptian man—among the sons of Israel, and they fought in the camp, the son of the Israelite woman and an Israelite man.
And the son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name and cursed. So they brought him to Moses. (And the name of his mother was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.)
And they placed him in custody, to be made clear to them according to the mouth of the Lord.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Bring out the blasphemer to outside the camp, and all who heard shall lay their hands on his head, and the whole assembly shall stone him.
And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: Any man who curses his God shall bear his sin.

And whoever blasphemes the name of the Lord shall surely be put to death; the whole assembly shall surely stone him. Whether foreigner or native, when he blasphemes the Name, he shall be put to death.
And a man who strikes any human life shall surely be put to death.
And whoever strikes the life of an animal shall make it good—life for life.
And a man who causes injury to his neighbor—as he has done, so shall it be done to him:
fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. As he has caused injury to a man, so shall it be done to him.
And whoever strikes an animal shall make it good, but whoever strikes a man shall be put to death.
One law shall be for you; as the foreigner so the native shall be, for I am the Lord your God.
And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel, and they brought out the blasphemer to outside the camp and stoned him with stones. And the sons of Israel did as the Lord commanded Moses.
